transcript.text: Okay, can you, can you hear me now?
Yeah, I can.
Okay, cool.
So first, let me know how to pronounce your name.
Feyi.
Feyi.
Ah, Feyi.
Feyi.
Cool.
Cool.
So, how's the onboarding been going?

Uh, I mean, it's been a thing for, um, getting the hang of other schools, because, um, I came from a freelance background, so I'm used to, um, not, I'm not very used to using a lot of these schools, like a lot of these are very new to me, but I'm definitely trying to catch up and ask questions.
My onboarding buddy, for example, I've asked so many questions, but yeah, I'm getting to do this.
Cool.
Yeah, it's definitely, even for people who have been in startups, it's definitely a lot of new tools, especially in the workflows and stuff.
I think a majority of the people haven't seen Aerofs and those types of things before coming to growthx, so I don't think it's anything to be worried about.
Okay.
yeah, tell me a little bit about your content background.
Okay, like I said, I used to freelance.
I used to work with agencies.
I used to write for different niches.
For example, this was about writing about tech.
Next, we were writing about travel.
Next, we writing about animals.
So, like, I have experience writing in different topics.
So, it's easy for me to, like, make deep research and adapt to whatever kind of things you're working with, basically.
So, yeah.
that's about that's the thing about my writing.
So, yeah, that's Thank you.
For about four years now, 2021.
Cool.
Awesome.
I've been doing this for a very long time.
I think I've been in content marketing for over 15 years now.
I've worked for agencies as well and also did a lot of in-house content work for startups, mostly like B2B SaaS startups from the U.S.
So I have lots of experience.
Jacob is also very experienced.
don't know if you've met with him yet.
He leads the pod that we're going to be a part of.
So, yeah, he's really good too.
And, you know, everyone, I how many people you've met now, but, you know, everyone is willing to help and willing to answer questions.
So, you know, don't be shy about shooting me a Slack message whenever you have any problems or anything you need to know.
Yeah, sure, no problem.
You're definitely an asker, so I'll be asking a question.
Where are you?
What was your location?
I'm currently in Serbia, so that's in Europe.
So it's good to have someone in my time zone because most everybody else is in the Americas or in North or South America.
I'm in Nigeria, so.
Yeah, so we're pretty close.
We work at around the same time, which is good.
Okay, I have a question about this information assignment.
Mm-hmm.
Okay, so I went through the client database.
I think I want to write for Rapid.
It's under J-Cog's client list.
So I went to your website, CIP Tech site, and I think we have some interesting info that we used to work.
So I'm a bit confused about the concept I'm supposed to do for them.
Am I supposed to write from a particular perspective or just like whatever I feel?
Am I supposed to write for like on the customers?
Am I supposed to write for like on the project employees?
What's it supposed to be like?
Are you talking about Rapid?
Yeah, Rapid.
Yeah, they're not one of my clients.
I don't know whose client they are.
So I have five points.
Is it from a particular perspective or am I just supposed to write based on how I'm feeling or how it's supposed to be like?
Oh, yeah, there's definitely, you're always going to be given, you know, a topic and there's always like a strategy.
I don't know what it concretely is for Rapid, but every single client, you know, we...
Put together a strategy and we always have like a brief that we give to writers.
So you always know what you have to write about.
You're not going to have to make it up.
What's that?
Sorry, I'm asking because this simulation assignments, they're not really given a topic.
They gave new writers an assignment just to get them on the go to write the simulation assignments for multiple clients that they choose to write for.
So it's not like, they're topic.
I think, I'm not sure, that's what I'm asking.
I'm not sure if I'm supposed to come up with a topic by myself or like how I'm supposed to write about this.
Okay.
For this task, that's for onboarding, I think you're supposed to kind of like come up with a task, with a subject yourself.
So kind of like take a look at their website, see what they do, and kind of like think of a topic.
And then it's not supposed to be like a blog post where it's like promoting Rapid or whatever company.
It's supposed to be like writing about a topic that goes with their products, whatever they do.

So I think it's maybe a little bit of keyword research, checking out what they work, what they do, what the product is, and then writing about like an adjacent topic that would make sense for them, something that would make sense to appear on their blog.
I didn't go through this onboarding, like I arrived before they had this, but I've looked at some of the writing from the last batch of people who were onboarded.

And I think that was like the key mistake was a lot of them were writing kind of like a salesy pitch about the company instead of writing about a topic, you know, that would make sense to talk about on their blog.
Thank very much.
Thank Thank Thank you.
Am I supposed to have access to TouchGPT for this?
Yeah.
That would be later on.
Yeah, I think everyone should have access to TouchGPT.
If you have one password, you should be able to find it.
The key is you have to sign in with the Teams, the Team account.
So it's team at growthx.
I don't know if you've met Teandra.
She just joined the pod like four weeks ago.
She's had to go through all of this kind of like onboarding stuff.
So she would be good to talk to as well.
I can connect you two because I think you're going to be working in the same pod.
And yeah, whenever you have any question about, you know, tools and stuff, you can just ask in the, you know, in flag and someone will be there to help you.
Thank you.
Okay.
Thanks.
Thank you.
I was supposed to have a meeting with Kyle today, but I think it has been postponed until next week, but it's going to affect my onboarding projects on Bolinia.
I'm supposed to complete the projects today, but because they postponed the meeting, I wouldn't be able to do that.
I don't know.
Because Christina has been messaging me that I'm supposed to complete everything today, but because of that, I wouldn't be able to complete it.
I'm not supposed to.
Oh, is the meeting with Kyle, is like a part of the onboarding?
Yeah.
Yeah.
I feel bad that I can't help you.
I don't have much info about the onboarding process.
So, do you have someone who's like leading that?
Sorry?
Sorry?
Who's the person that's like leading the onboarding for you?
Like, who are you talking to most?
I'm talking to a lot of people, actually.
I've been talking with Christina, my own body is Rachel, so I've been speaking with her a lot, and Jacob.
So just everybody.
Okay.
Okay.
So, yeah, I really don't, I don't know about the onboarding task, literally, and all that stuff.
So, it would probably be a better task than one of us.
If you haven't, like, I'm more here if you want to have, like, if have some general questions about what the workflow looks like, you know, what the clients are like, you know, stuff like that.
What clients do you look at the moment?
Okay.
So, right now, I have about five clients.
One is Aftershoot, which is like an app for editing and calling photos.
So, you know, audience is like a wedding.
Photographers, things like that, it's pretty good.
The writing's kind of like very playful and fun, but they have like very kind of like strict guidelines and suggestions.
So even though it's kind of like a fun thing to write about, they usually ask for a lot of edits.
So we're kind of moving pretty slowly with them.
We've only published there on like 20 articles so far.
But, you know, they're okay.
They're pretty okay to work with.
I have the easiest client I would have this right now is Sunbit.
Kind of writing like very basic articles for them about like the dental industry and about like auto mechanics and stuff like that.
So it's articles like, you know, what is a dental filling?
You know, how much do dental crowns cost?
You know, car noise.
Those you should be aware of, how much does it cost to fix your fuel pump, things like that.
Because their company, they do financing.
If you need dental surgery right away and you don't have the money, you can go finance it through them.
They'll pay for it and they'll paying back in installments.
So that's their business model.
So we're writing articles for Topic Funnel for people, you know, you have, you know, a cavity or you have pain in your tooth.
Maybe search an article about that, read a little bit about that, and then find out that you can sign dentists through something.
And then the other ones are more technical.
We have Shaped, Airbyte, and the newest one is Ambit.
So they're really kind of technical.
One is like data warehousing.
One is kind of like AI-led personalization and customization, and the third one is just more like an AI security thing.
So they're like really technical.
I don't know how much experience you have with like those types of companies.
I mean, like I said, I actually do enjoy doing research.
So even if it's, you know, something I've done before, can easily just catch up on it.
I wanted to ask, I went through some of the, I went through all of the videos actually, AI SEO videos that are from Maseo Odedey.
I don't know if you've ever like have to watch them.

And I wanted to ask, because I'm not used to writing with AI tools and all of that, it's usually just write from scratch and it kind of tells you what to do, and then you start to make your own personal research and write from the beginning.
So how do I integrate those AI tools?
Am I supposed to...

I'm to come up with the articles like CRC and Publix CC, I'm not supposed to use them to come up with the entire articles, or just use them for the outlines, then work with my own content, what process is it supposed to be like?
It really depends on, like, usually we want to use the workflows, right?
The ones that we're creating.
So that's like, in the workflow, all you need to put in is the name of the company, the keyword you're targeting, and like that AI context about the company.
And then the workflow will do everything else for you.
It'll create an outline, do the research, create a title, and then create an article draft.
And then you will take that article draft, and you will start editing it down.
And you can, I mean, we all, I think, use different things sometimes.
The workflow doesn't give you exactly something you can work with.
And sometimes it's better to just work with ChatGPT independently.
Like, I'll do that.

Like, I'll go to ChatGPT and I'll tell it, you know, here's the client, here's what they want, here's a little bit about them, and then here's the topic, and I'll ask for an outline first, and then I'll work on the outline with ChatGPT, and once the outline is good, I'll say, okay, let's start writing it, you know, section by section.
So sometimes that works better, sometimes the workflow is good.
I think you'll find out as you go along, when you start, you know, using it, you'll find out what works best for you, and kind of adopt that.
Ideally, you know, the plan is that the workflow is supposed to, you know, get a lot better as we hire more developers and work on that.

So, you know, that's the hope that, you know, in six months, you know, or in a year, you know, we're going to be using nothing but the workflow, and it's going to give us really good results every time.
That, you know, we'll just have to be, you know, edited instead of, like, having to rewrite everything.
Thank you.
I think that's the question to ask for now, I'll be able to reach out to you, and you'd have enough time to answer any other questions.
Of course, yeah, I'm on Slack, shoot me a message anytime you have.
Thank you.
Alright, thanks a lot.
Nice to meet you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory meeting between David Galic and new hire Feyisayo Odedeyi to discuss onboarding progress and content writing processes at growthx.ai.](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=2.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Feyi's onboarding involves learning new tools; David reassures it's normal and encourages questions](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=32.0)
  - [David has 15+ years of content marketing experience; offers support for Feyi's transition](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)
  - [Clarified simulation assignment approach: choose topic based on client research, write adjacent content](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0)
  - [Workflow tools (e.g., TouchGPT) discussed; flexibility in AI-assisted content creation emphasized](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=794.0)

## Topics

### Onboarding Progress and Tool Familiarization

  - [Feyi adapting to new tools from freelance background; finding it challenging but actively learning](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=32.0)
  - [David reassures: most new hires unfamiliar with tools like Aerofs](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=71.0)
  - [Encourages Feyi to ask questions freely, emphasizing team's willingness to help](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)

### Content Writing Backgrounds

  - [Feyi: 4 years freelance experience across diverse niches (tech, travel, animals)](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=88.0)
  - [David: 15+ years in content marketing, agency and in-house experience, focus on B2B SaaS](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)
  - [Jacob (pod leader): highly experienced, though not present in meeting](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=157.0)

### Simulation Assignment Clarification

  - [Feyi confused about approach for Rapid (client) simulation](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=242.0)
  - [David advises:](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
    1.  Research client's website and offerings
    2.  Conduct keyword research
    3.  Choose adjacent topic relevant to client's blog
    4.  Avoid salesy pitches; focus on informative content

### AI Tools in Content Creation

  - [TouchGPT access confirmed (via Teams account: team@growthx)](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=430.0)
  - [Workflow tool usage explained:](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=794.0)
    1.  Input company name, target keyword, AI context
    2.  AI generates outline, research, title, and draft
    3.  Writer edits and refines AI-generated content
  - [Flexibility emphasized: Use ChatGPT directly if workflow results unsatisfactory](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=839.0)
  - [Future improvements to workflow tool anticipated](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=872.0)

### Client Overview

[David's current clients:](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=582.0)

1.  Aftershoot: Photo editing app (20 articles published, playful tone, strict guidelines)
2.  Sunbit: Financing for dental/auto services (basic, informative articles)
3.  Shaped, Airbyte, Ambit: Technical clients (AI, data warehousing, security)

### Logistical Challenges

  - [Feyi's meeting with Kyle postponed, impacting onboarding project completion](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=481.0)
  - [David unable to assist directly with onboarding logistics](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Feyi to connect with Teandra (recent hire) for onboarding insights](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)
  - [Feyi to reach out to Christina or Rachel regarding onboarding timeline adjustments](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=503.0)
  - [Feyi to explore AI tools and workflows, finding best personal approach](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=794.0)
  - [David available on Slack for future questions and support](https://fathom.video/share/z9EuHmEQpgNoGL9-xAjAzscMWxKBSenz?tab=summary&timestamp=570.0)

action_items.description: Complete simulation assignment for Rapid - research, choose topic, write non-promotional blog post
Access TouchGPT via Teams account (team@growthx) in 1Password
Connect Feyisayo with Teandra for onboarding support
